essence: David locked in HyperX QuadCast settings (gain 2.5-3, input volume 5/7, mic angled up) and asked they be documented, critiqued the C18 video intro for opening on a full-screen graphic instead of on-camera footage against brand style, and flagged a workflow-engine bug where the dev-tools output panel stays visible after switching from debug to user mode.

actions.text: Document final HyperX QuadCast gain/input-volume settings
Fix C18 intro to start on-camera per brand style and research pattern-break timing formulas
Investigate dev-tools panel remaining visible after debug-to-user mode switch and review debug-only enablement system
Check/create unit test best-practices doc and decide tests for new component boundary

loop.label: workflow engine debug panel and C18 intro fixes

essence: Running FliHub videos C15-C18 through the proof-of-concept workflow engine, David flagged that 'load from paste' does not reset the workflow to show new data (forcing refresh and data loss), critiqued raw JSON strings not mapping to structured CTA/affiliate/social objects, and requested JSON-editor research plus textarea-sizing documentation, coordinating via an orchestrator pattern with vDave's agent Alex.

actions.text: Document how textarea/grower sizing is determined in workflow docs
Research JSON editor components and plan integration into workflow UI and schemas
Add minor UI hint marking JSON fields even before a real editor component exists

essence: David told the agent a different orchestrator would be issuing its instructions, asked what handoff knowledge it needs (Pong UI connection, YouTube launch optimizer awareness), and mandated that all responses include absolute filesystem paths.

actions.text: Always include absolute filesystem paths in agent responses

essence: David tried to recall Ian's mention of running a Java/JavaScript program as an always-on service (not from a terminal) via some tool or pattern, and wished his OMI device were connected to a second-brain system so such details were retrievable.
David asked the agent to retain the automation knowledge just explained and capture it in a small starter document to return to later.

actions.text: Create a starter document capturing the discussed automation knowledge

essence: David wanted button-deck automations that launch and position WhatsApp/Finder/VS Code/Chrome/iTerm/Zoom/Ecamm per monitor folded into the agentic OS, critiqued RADAR's readability, and added two backlog app ideas — a Claude conversation-hooks watcher (AngelEye precursor) and a dedicated todo app using the new HTML-style layout — insisting on separating in-development apps from radar-backlog apps.

actions.text: Add app launch/position automations (WhatsApp, Finder, VS Code, Chrome, iTerm, Zoom, Ecamm) to button deck and integrate into agentic OS
Add Claude conversation-hooks watcher app to radar backlog
Add dedicated todo app concept to radar backlog with the new HTML-based layout

essence: David named the new personal-organization skill RADAR (rejecting 'morning brief' and 'daily ops' while keeping 'what's my morning brief' as an invocation, with today.md plus summary output), then hit SSH cross-machine access failures on the M4 Pro/M2 Mini and asked for a dedicated skill so Claude reliably remembers it can reach other machines.

Add today.md contents plus brief summary output option to RADAR

essence: David designed what focusing the todo/inbox brain should show — a nicely formatted concept overview with a primary view of core items and a secondary list beyond eight — noting that defining what belongs in that list is itself knowledge to preserve, and that a ChatGPT agent-workflow conversation should be saved to markdown.

actions.text: Save ChatGPT agent-workflow conversation into markdown research doc
Define primary/secondary overview format shown when focusing the todo brain

essence: David studied a smoothie shop's menu architecture built on six ingredient roles (fruit base, ice/water carrier, dairy, etc.), praising its deliberate simplicity as a model for the Joy Juice menu system.

David and Joy settled the Joy Juice tagline 'Two styles, one joy' (Joy Juice Chiang Mai), decided on high-quality plastic packaging with QR codes and PromptPay scan-to-pay, and requested a third image prompt plus a NotebookLM style-guide prompt showing the eight fruits side by side.

actions.text: Create image prompt three and a style-guide prompt listing the eight fruits side by side

essence: David designed the Joy Juice bilingual menu system — Thai-style vs Farang/healthier-style drinks per fruit with feeling words like 'aroi mak mak', eight fruits in side-by-side comparison tables, a per-fruit JSON menu schema documented in Schema.md, and a NotebookLM export with a style prompt insert.

actions.text: Pick eight fruits and build Thai/English side-by-side comparison table with per-fruit menu JSON schema
Document brand and menu schemas in Schema.md
Export all menu and branding docs to a single NotebookLM-importable file with style prompt

essence: David decided the Joy Juice menu data should be structured as menu.json instead of markdown with a small HTML/JS rendering tool, and asked for a style analysis of two downloaded menu images so the visual style (FliLang-style health vs Thai-style taste/tradition theming) can be reproduced.

actions.text: Convert menu data to menu.json and build an HTML/JS rendering capability
Import the two downloaded menu images and describe their visual style for reproduction

essence: The Joy Juice shop opens tomorrow in a roughly 3.5m by 2.5m space, and David asked for an absolute filesystem path where he can drop found images for the shop's settings and configuration.

actions.text: Provide an absolute path / Finder location for dropping Joy Juice images
